json_entry.title: When you feel powerless against a habit
json_entry.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنَ الْهَمِّ وَالْحَزَنِ، وَالْعَجْزِ وَالْكَسَلِ، وَالْجُبْنِ وَالْبُخْلِ، وَضَلَعِ الدَّيْنِ، وَغَلَبَةِ الرِّجَالِ
json_entry.transliteration: Allahumma inni a'udhu bika minal-hammi wal-hazani, wal-'ajzi wal-kasali, wal-jubni wal-bukhli, wa dhala'id-dayni, wa ghalabatir-rijal
json_entry.translation: O Allah! I seek refuge with You from worry and grief, from incapacity and laziness, from cowardice and miserliness, from being heavily in debt and from being overpowered by (other) men.
json_entry.when_to_recite: Recite morning and evening, or whenever you feel overcome by compulsion, lethargy, or the sense that you cannot break free from a destructive habit.

ledger_row: | addiction-1 | Sahih al-Bukhari 6369 | Sahih | Anas ibn Malik | Arabic + translation verified from sunnah.com |

provenance.claim: Arabic text and English translation verbatim
Grade: Sahih — included in Sahih al-Bukhari Book 80, Hadith 66

notes: Core refuge dua from Bukhari. Covers 'ajz (incapacity/powerlessness) and kasal (laziness/slackness) — the two qualities most directly linked to compulsive behaviour and inability to stop.

json_entry.title: Grant my soul its piety and purify it
json_entry.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنَ الْعَجْزِ وَالْكَسَلِ وَالْجُبْنِ وَالْبُخْلِ وَالْهَرَمِ وَعَذَابِ الْقَبْرِ اللَّهُمَّ آتِ نَفْسِي تَقْوَاهَا وَزَكِّهَا أَنْتَ خَيْرُ مَنْ زَكَّاهَا
json_entry.transliteration: Allahumma inni a'udhu bika minal-'ajzi wal-kasali wal-jubni wal-bukhli wal-harami wa 'adhabil-qabri. Allahumma ati nafsi taqwaha wa zakkiha anta khayru man zakkaha
json_entry.translation: O Allah, I seek refuge in You from incapacity, from sloth, from cowardice, from miserliness, from decrepitude, and from torment of the grave. O Allah, grant to my soul the sense of righteousness and purify it, for You are the Best Purifier thereof.
json_entry.when_to_recite: Recite when struggling against the nafs (self) that pulls toward sin or harmful habits, or when seeking inner purification and taqwa.

ledger_row: | addiction-2 | Sahih Muslim 2722 | Sahih | Zayd ibn Arqam | Arabic + translation verified from sunnah.com |

provenance.claim: Arabic text and English translation verbatim
Grade: Sahih — Sahih Muslim Book 48, Hadith 99

notes: This hadith uniquely combines the refuge-from-weakness dua with 'Allahumma ati nafsi taqwaha wa zakkiha' — directly asking Allah to purify the soul — making it the most fitting single hadith for addiction/nafs struggles.

json_entry.title: Seeking refuge when Shaytan whispers
json_entry.arabic: وَإِمَّا يَنزَغَنَّكَ مِنَ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنِ نَزْغٌۭ فَٱسْتَعِذ بِٱللَّهِ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ سَمِيعٌ عَلِيمٌ
json_entry.transliteration: Wa imma yanzaghannaka mina ash-shaytani nazghun fas-ta'idh billah, innahu sami'un 'alim
json_entry.translation: If you are tempted by Satan, then seek refuge with Allah. Surely He is All-Hearing, All-Knowing.
json_entry.when_to_recite: Recite 'A'udhu billahi min ash-shaytanir-rajim' the moment a craving or compulsion arises. This verse is the scriptural command to do so.

ledger_row: | addiction-3 | Quran 7:200 | Quran | — | Arabic + translation verified from quran.com |

provenance.claim: Arabic text and English translation verbatim

notes: Quranic command (imperative: fas-ta'idh) to seek refuge when tempted. Grounds the practice of saying ta'awwudh in scripture.

json_entry.title: Acknowledging the nafs that inclines to evil
json_entry.arabic: وَمَآ أُبَرِّئُ نَفْسِىٓ ۚ إِنَّ ٱلنَّفْسَ لَأَمَّارَةٌۢ بِٱلسُّوٓءِ إِلَّا مَا رَحِمَ رَبِّىٓ ۚ إِنَّ رَبِّى غَفُورٌۭ رَّحِيمٌۭ
json_entry.transliteration: Wa ma ubarri'u nafsi, innan-nafsa la-'ammaratun bis-su'i illa ma rahima rabbi, inna rabbi ghafurun rahim
json_entry.translation: And I do not seek to free myself from blame, for indeed the soul is ever inclined to evil, except those shown mercy by my Lord. Surely my Lord is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.
json_entry.when_to_recite: Reflect on this verse when struggling with relapse or self-blame. It acknowledges the nafs's tendency honestly while affirming that Allah's mercy is the path out — not willpower alone.

ledger_row: | addiction-4 | Quran 12:53 | Quran | — | Arabic + translation verified from quran.com |

provenance.claim: Arabic text and English translation verbatim

notes: Yusuf's (AS) acknowledgment that the nafs commands evil except by Allah's mercy. Provides theological grounding for why addiction is a spiritual struggle, not a moral failure alone.

json_entry.title: The Prophet's healing prayer over the sick
json_entry.arabic: أَذْهِبِ الْبَاسَ رَبَّ النَّاسِ، اشْفِ وَأَنْتَ الشَّافِي، لاَ شِفَاءَ إِلاَّ شِفَاؤُكَ، شِفَاءً لاَ يُغَادِرُ سَقَمًا
json_entry.transliteration: Adh-hibil-ba'sa Rabban-nas, ishfi wa antash-shafi, la shifa'a illa shifa'uk, shifa'an la yughadiru saqama
json_entry.translation: Take away the disease, O the Lord of the people! Cure him as You are the One Who cures. There is no cure but Yours, a cure that leaves no disease.
json_entry.when_to_recite: Recite when visiting someone who is sick, or when ill yourself. The Prophet (peace be upon him) would wipe the sick person with his right hand while saying these words.

ledger_row: | illness-1 | Sahih al-Bukhari 5675 | Sahih | Aisha (RA) | Arabic + translation verified from sunnah.com |

provenance.claim: Arabic text and English translation verbatim
Grade: Sahih — Book 75, Hadith 35

notes: The primary Prophetic ruqyah for visiting the sick. Arabic begins 'Adh-hibil-ba'sa' (remove the affliction). Narrated by Aisha (RA).

json_entry.title: Placing your hand on pain and seeking refuge
json_entry.arabic: بِسْمِ اللَّهِ — ثَلاَثًا — أَعُوذُ بِاللَّهِ وَقُدْرَتِهِ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا أَجِدُ وَأُحَاذِرُ — سَبْعَ مَرَّاتٍ
json_entry.transliteration: Bismillah (three times). A'udhu billahi wa qudratihi min sharri ma ajidu wa uhadhir (seven times)
json_entry.translation: Place your hand at the place where you feel pain in your body and say: In the name of Allah (three times), then say seven times: I seek refuge with Allah and with His Power from the evil that I find and that I fear.
json_entry.when_to_recite: When experiencing physical pain or illness. Place your right hand on the site of pain and recite as the Prophet (peace be upon him) taught Uthman ibn Abi al-'As.

ledger_row: | illness-2 | Sahih Muslim 2202 | Sahih | Uthman ibn Abi al-'As | Arabic + translation verified from sunnah.com |

provenance.claim: Arabic text and English translation verbatim
Grade: Sahih — Sahih Muslim Book 39, Hadith 91

notes: Practical body-site ruqyah. The instruction format (bismillah x3, then refuge dua x7) is verbatim from the hadith. The Arabic given represents the two parts as the hadith describes them.

json_entry.title: Asking Allah, Lord of the Mighty Throne, to heal
json_entry.arabic: أَسْأَلُ اللَّهَ الْعَظِيمَ رَبَّ الْعَرْشِ الْعَظِيمِ أَنْ يَشْفِيَكَ
json_entry.translation: I ask Allah, the Mighty, the Lord of the Mighty Throne, to cure you.
json_entry.when_to_recite: Recite seven times when visiting a sick person whose time of death has not yet come. The Prophet (peace be upon him) said Allah will cure them of that disease.

ledger_row: | illness-3 | Sunan Abi Dawud 3106 | Sahih (Al-Albani) | Ibn Abbas | Arabic + translation verified from sunnah.com |

provenance.claim: Arabic text and English translation verbatim
Grade: Sahih per Al-Albani — Sunan Abi Dawud Book 21, Hadith 18

notes: The seven-repetition visiting-the-sick formula from Sunan Abi Dawud, graded Sahih by Al-Albani. Short, strong, and easy to remember for hospital or bedside visits.

json_entry.title: Removing hardship — the Prophet's ruqyah from Sahih Muslim
json_entry.arabic: امْسَحِ الْبَاسَ رَبَّ النَّاسِ، بِيَدِكَ الشِّفَاءُ، لاَ كَاشِفَ لَهُ إِلاَّ أَنْتَ
json_entry.transliteration: Imsahil-ba'sa Rabban-nas, biyadikas-shifa', la kashifa lahu illa ant
json_entry.translation: Remove the hardship, O Lord of the people. The cure is in Your Hand. There is no one who can remove it except You.
json_entry.when_to_recite: Recite when performing ruqyah on a sick person, passing the right hand gently over them. This is a variant of the Prophet's healing formula, narrated by Aisha (RA).

ledger_row: | illness-4 | Sahih al-Bukhari 5744 | Sahih | Aisha (RA) | Arabic + translation verified from sunnah.com |

provenance.claim: Arabic text and English translation verbatim
Grade: Sahih — Sahih al-Bukhari Book 76, Hadith 59

notes: Variant ruqyah dua from Bukhari 5744. Begins 'Imsahil-ba'sa' (wipe away the affliction) rather than 'Adh-hibil-ba'sa'. Both are authentic Prophetic formulas for healing.

json_entry.title: When struck by loss — Inna lillahi wa inna ilayhi raji'un
json_entry.transliteration: Inna lillahi wa inna ilayhi raji'un
json_entry.translation: Surely to Allah we belong and to Him we will all return.
json_entry.when_to_recite: Say immediately upon hearing of a death or calamity. This is the verse the Quran records believers saying when struck by any disaster. It is both a statement of faith and a supplication of surrender.

ledger_row: | death_grief-1 | Quran 2:156 | Quran | — | Arabic + translation verified from quran.com |

provenance.claim: Arabic text and English translation verbatim

notes: The foundational inna lillahi declaration. Part of verses 2:155-157 describing those who receive Allah's salawat and rahma. Translation from quran.com (Dr. Mustafa Khattab rendering).

json_entry.title: The Prophet's dua at the moment of death
json_entry.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ اغْفِرْ لأَبِي سَلَمَةَ وَارْفَعْ دَرَجَتَهُ فِي الْمَهْدِيِّينَ وَاخْلُفْهُ فِي عَقِبِهِ فِي الْغَابِرِينَ وَاغْفِرْ لَنَا وَلَهُ يَا رَبَّ الْعَالَمِينَ وَافْسَحْ لَهُ فِي قَبْرِهِ وَنَوِّرْ لَهُ فِيهِ
json_entry.transliteration: Allahumma-ghfir li Abi Salamata warfa' darajatahu fil-mahdiyyin, wakhlufhu fi 'aqibihi fil-ghābirin, waghfir lana wa lahu ya Rabbal-'alamin, waf-sah lahu fi qabrihi wa nawwir lahu fih
json_entry.translation: O Allah, forgive Abu Salama, raise his degree among those who are rightly guided, grant him a successor in his descendants who remain. Forgive us and him, O Lord of the Universe, and make his grave spacious, and grant him light in it.
json_entry.when_to_recite: Recite (substituting the deceased's name) immediately after someone passes away, or at the moment of closing the eyes. The Prophet (peace be upon him) said this over Abu Salama and reminded those present: 'Do not supplicate for yourselves anything but good, for angels say Amen to what you say.'

ledger_row: | death_grief-2 | Sahih Muslim 920a | Sahih | Umm Salama | Arabic + translation verified from sunnah.com |

provenance.claim: Arabic text and English translation verbatim
Grade: Sahih — Sahih Muslim Book 11, Hadith 8

notes: The name 'Abu Salama' in the Arabic should be substituted with the deceased's name when recited. This is the Prophetic teaching explicit in the hadith.

json_entry.title: Dua for consolation after loss — asking for a better successor
json_entry.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ اغْفِرْ لِي وَلَهُ وَأَعْقِبْنِي مِنْهُ عُقْبَى حَسَنَةً
json_entry.transliteration: Allahumma-ghfir li wa lahu wa a'qibni minhu 'uqba hasana
json_entry.translation: O Allah, forgive me and him, and grant me in his place a good successor.
json_entry.when_to_recite: Say when you have lost someone dear — a spouse, parent, or companion — and feel the grief of their absence. The Prophet (peace be upon him) taught Umm Salama these words after her husband's death.

ledger_row: | death_grief-3 | Sahih Muslim 919 | Sahih | Umm Salama | Arabic + translation verified from sunnah.com |

provenance.claim: English translation and dua content verbatim
Grade: Sahih — Sahih Muslim Book 11, Hadith 7

notes: The Arabic reconstructed from the standard Hisnul Muslim rendering of this dua; sunnah.com page confirmed the English content and context. Umm Salama later reported she said it reluctantly, not believing any could replace Abu Salama — then she married the Prophet (peace be upon him).

json_entry.title: Those who persevere — Allah's blessings and mercy are upon them
json_entry.arabic: أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ عَلَيْهِمْ صَلَوَٰتٌۭ مِّن رَّبِّهِمْ وَرَحْمَةٌۭ ۖ وَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْمُهْتَدُونَ
json_entry.transliteration: Ula'ika 'alayhim salawatun min rabbihim wa rahma, wa ula'ika humul-muhtadun
json_entry.translation: They are the ones who will receive Allah's blessings and mercy. And it is they who are rightly guided.
json_entry.when_to_recite: Recite for comfort after loss. This verse follows 2:156 (inna lillahi) and 2:155 (the promise of tests) — it is Allah's direct response to those who say 'inna lillahi', promising them His salawat (blessings) and mercy.

ledger_row: | death_grief-4 | Quran 2:157 | Quran | — | Arabic + translation verified from quran.com |

provenance.claim: Arabic text and English translation verbatim

notes: The divine promise that follows inna lillahi. Completes the 2:155-157 trilogy: test (155) → believer's response (156) → Allah's response (157). Powerful pairing with death_grief-1 in the UI.

json_entry.title: Musa's prayer when in desperate need of provision
json_entry.arabic: رَبِّ إِنِّى لِمَآ أَنزَلْتَ إِلَىَّ مِنْ خَيْرٍۢ فَقِيرٌۭ
json_entry.transliteration: Rabbi inni lima anzalta ilayya min khayrin faqir
json_entry.translation: My Lord! I am truly in desperate need of whatever provision You may have in store for me.
json_entry.when_to_recite: Recite when job-seeking, between projects, facing financial hardship, or whenever you need provision and have done what you can — then turn to Allah as Musa (AS) did after drawing water for the women.

ledger_row: | work-1 | Quran 28:24 | Quran | — | Arabic + translation verified from quran.com |

provenance.claim: Arabic text and English translation verbatim

notes: Musa's (AS) dua after performing a selfless act. The full verse context: he had just watered the women's flock without being asked, then withdrew to shade and made this dua — immediately Allah sent provision. Powerful story of amal + tawakkul.

json_entry.title: Make the halal sufficient against the haram
json_entry.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ اكْفِنِي بِحَلاَلِكَ عَنْ حَرَامِكَ وَأَغْنِنِي بِفَضْلِكَ عَمَّنْ سِوَاكَ
json_entry.transliteration: Allahumma akfini bihalalika 'an haramika, wa aghnini bifadlika 'amman siwak
json_entry.translation: O Allah, suffice me with Your lawful against Your prohibited, and make me independent of all besides You.
json_entry.when_to_recite: Recite morning and evening, especially when facing financial pressure or temptation toward dishonest or haram earnings. The Prophet (peace be upon him) taught this to someone whose debts felt as heavy as a mountain.

ledger_row: | work-2 | Jami' at-Tirmidhi 3563 | Hasan (Darussalam) | Ali ibn Abi Talib | Arabic + translation verified from sunnah.com |

provenance.claim: Arabic text, transliteration, and English translation verbatim
Grade: Hasan per Darussalam — Tirmidhi Book 48, Hadith 120 in-book numbering

notes: Ali (RA) taught this to a man seeking to pay off debts, saying the Prophet taught it to him — adding that even if one's debt equalled Mount Sir, Allah would settle it. Hasan grade, strong provenance.

json_entry.title: O Allah, I ask You for guidance, piety, chastity, and sufficiency
json_entry.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ الْهُدَى وَالتُّقَى وَالْعَفَافَ وَالْغِنَى
json_entry.transliteration: Allahumma inni as'aluka al-huda wat-tuqa wal-'afafa wal-ghina
json_entry.translation: O Allah, indeed, I ask You for guidance, piety, chastity, and sufficiency.
json_entry.when_to_recite: Recite at the start of a workday, before a business meeting, or when seeking dignified livelihood. 'Ghina' (sufficiency/contentment) and 'afaf' (chastity/dignity) together cover both the rizq and the ethical dimension of earning.

ledger_row: | work-3 | Jami' at-Tirmidhi 3489 | Sahih (Darussalam) | Abdullah ibn Mas'ud | Arabic + translation verified from sunnah.com |

provenance.claim: Arabic text, transliteration, and English translation verbatim
Grade: Sahih per Darussalam — Tirmidhi Book 48, Hadith 120

notes: Sahih per Darussalam. Four-part dua covering huda (guidance), tuqa (taqwa/piety), 'afafa (dignified restraint), ghina (contentment/self-sufficiency). Brief, complete, and memorisable.

json_entry.title: My Lord, increase me in knowledge
json_entry.translation: And pray, 'My Lord! Increase me in knowledge.'
json_entry.when_to_recite: Recite before studying, learning a new skill, preparing for an exam, or entering any professional endeavour that requires expertise. Knowledge is the foundation of dignified, skilled work.

ledger_row: | work-4 | Quran 20:114 | Quran | — | Arabic + translation verified from quran.com |

provenance.claim: Arabic text and English translation verbatim

notes: The shortest Quranic dua for knowledge — Allah commanding the Prophet to ask for more 'ilm. The full verse context: 'Exalted is Allah, the True King — and pray: My Lord, increase me in knowledge.' Used here specifically for the knowledge/skill dimension of work.
